item.title: 资金涌入新兴市场ETF，股市遭遇崩盘冲击
item.description: 本周，尽管市场剧烈震荡，投资者仍持续向投资新兴市场的交易所交易基金注资，这表明他们相信中东战争不会长期动摇风险资产。【原文：Investors kept putting money into exchange-traded funds that invest in emerging-markets this week even as markets convulsed, indicating confidence that the war in the Middle East won’t roil risk assets for long.】

item.title: 美国化工股有望因战争引发的价格上涨而受益
item.description: 分析师指出，美国上市化工股有望受益于伊朗战争引发的供应紧缩和价格上涨。【原文：US-listed chemicals stocks are likely to benefit from tighter supply and higher prices fueled by the war with Iran, according to analysts.】

item.title: 谷歌Pixel 10a迭代幅度过小，即便售价499美元也不值得推荐
item.description: 谷歌售价499美元的Pixel 10a智能手机未能在聚光灯下停留太久。就在该设备3月5日发售日前夕，苹果公司本周宣布推出售价599美元的iPhone 17e，该机型将于3月11日正式上市。【原文：Google’s $499 Pixel 10a smartphone didn’t get to enjoy the spotlight for long. This week, just days before the device’s March 5 release date, Apple Inc. announced the $599 iPhone 17e, which goes on sale March 11.】

item.title: 欧盟遴选瑞典、英国企业管理50亿欧元科技基金
item.description: 欧盟计划设立一项基金，用于资助从事量子计算、人工智能及其他尖端技术领域的公司，目前已从法国、瑞典和英国遴选出多家管理公司进入候选名单。【原文：A planned European Union fund to finance companies working on quantum computing, artificial intelligence and other deep tech fields has shortlisted management firms from France, Sweden and the UK.】

item.title: 贝克休斯寻求发行100亿美元跨境债券
item.description: 知情人士透露，贝克休斯公司计划通过跨境债券发行筹集约100亿美元资金，用于收购Chart Industries公司。【原文：Baker Hughes Co. is planning to raise about $10 billion from a cross-border bond sale to help fund its acquisition of Chart Industries Inc., according to people with knowledge of the matter.】

item.title: 银湖资本支持的UFC、WWE所有者获得近10亿美元贷款
item.description: 据知情人士透露，在周三宣布向股权持有者派发股息后，运营UFC和WWE的TKO集团控股公司已在杠杆贷款市场发起近10亿美元的融资。【原文：UFC and WWE operator TKO Group Holdings, Inc. launched a nearly $1 billion offering in the leveraged loan market, according to a person familiar with the transaction, after the company announced a dividend to equity holders on Wednesday.】

item.title: 伊朗在美军空袭两天后仍继续在哈尔格岛装运原油
item.description: 伊朗周一继续在其哈尔格岛石油终端向油轮装载原油，此前两天美国和以色列对这个波斯湾国家发动了空袭。随着冲突升级，目前尚不清楚这种装载能否持续。【原文：Iran continued loading crude onto tankers at its Kharg Island oil terminal on Monday, two days after the US and Israel launched air attacks against the Persian Gulf country. It’s unclear whether that can continue as the conflict has intensified.】

item.title: 俄亥俄天主教学院因面临赤字未能支付三月债券款项
item.description: 俄亥俄多米尼加大学未能支付3月1日到期的债券款项——这是该校财务困境的最新迹象。【原文：Ohio Dominican University didn’t make a bond payment due on March 1 — the latest sign of the school’s financial woes.】

item.title: Neura Robotics在Tether支持的融资轮中筹集10亿欧元
item.description: 德国初创企业Neura Robotics正筹集约10亿欧元（12亿美元）资金，本轮融资由稳定币发行商Tether Holdings SA支持，旨在开发人工智能驱动的人形机器人。【原文：German startup Neura Robotics is raising about €1 billion ($1.2 billion) in a funding round backed by stablecoin issuer Tether Holdings SA to develop an artificial-intelligence-powered humanoid robot.】

item.title: 高盛进一步改善杜邦子公司出售贷款条款
item.description: 高盛集团进一步改善了与化工制造商Arclin公司收购杜邦公司芳纶业务相关的12.5亿美元融资条款，该融资规模已缩减。此举正值杠杆贷款市场持续承压之际。【原文：Goldman Sachs Group Inc. sweetened terms further on a downsized $1.25 billion of financing tied to chemical maker Arclin Inc.’s acquisition of DuPont de Nemours Inc.’s Aramids business amid ongoing struggles for leveraged loans.】

item.title: 霍尔木兹海峡航运陷入瘫痪
item.description: 在美以与伊朗的战争中，通过重要航道霍尔木兹海峡的交通几乎陷入瘫痪。【原文：Traffic through the vital Strait of Hormuz shipping strait has reached a near standstill amid the war between the US, Israel, and Iran.】

item.title: 麦克莱姆称加拿大央行将转向中央回购清算
item.description: 蒂夫·麦克勒姆行长宣布加拿大央行将启动回购市场的中央清算机制，同时他阐述了该国金融体系面临的下行风险。【原文：Governor Tiff Macklem announced that the Bank of Canada would start central clearing of repo markets as he outlined downside risks to the country’s financial system.】

item.title: 欧盟因关税不确定性冻结美欧贸易协议
item.description: 在法院裁定特朗普总统的全球性关税无效后，欧洲议会决定继续暂停批准欧盟与美国的贸易协定，该协定将保持冻结状态。【原文：The European Union’s trade deal with the US will stay frozen after the European Parliament decided to keep its ratification paused in the wake of a court ruling invalidating President Donald Trump’s global tariffs.】

item.title: 亲身体验：苹果新款iPhone 17e及其他新品
item.description: 苹果公司本周发布了一系列新品，包括售价599美元的MacBook Neo——这是该公司首款真正意义上的低端笔记本电脑——以及iPhone 17e。该公司还宣布了MacBook Pro、MacBook Air、Studio Display和iPad Air的更新版本。【原文：Apple Inc. this week unveiled a slate of new products, including the $599 MacBook Neo — its first true low-end laptop — and the iPhone 17e. The company also announced updated versions of the MacBook Pro, MacBook Air, Studio Display and iPad Air.】

item.title: 伊朗战争冲击非洲美元债券，投资者纷纷撤离
item.description: 随着伊朗战争推动投资者涌向美元避险，非洲美元债券利差持续扩大，导致整个非洲大陆的借贷成本上升。【原文：Spreads on African dollar bonds are widening as the Iran war drives investors into the safety of the US dollar, lifting borrowing costs across the continent.】

item.title: Citadel证券分析师鲁布纳撤回看跌预测，预计三月将出现反弹
item.description: 在美国对伊朗开战引发股市剧烈波动之际，城堡证券的斯科特·鲁布纳表示，其对市场的基本面分析显示，当前正是看涨股市的时机。【原文：As US stock investors endure bouts of volatility in the wake of the US war on Iran, Citadel Securities’ Scott Rubner said his fundamental analysis of the market signals now is a time to turn bullish on equities.】

item.title: 霍尔木兹海峡封锁致中东地区石油储备迅速填满
item.description: 中东地区的石油生产商正面临紧张倒计时，伊朗战争阻断了该地区的主要出口通道，导致各国储油罐满溢，若局势持续将威胁减产。【原文：Oil producers across the Middle East face a tense countdown as the Iran war blocks the region’s main export artery, filling the countries’ storage tanks and threatening output cuts if the situation persists.】

item.title: 美联储调查显示：2025年企业缩减薪资支出，医疗成本却大幅攀升
item.description: 根据纽约联邦储备银行最近的一项调查，2025年企业面临着近年来最剧烈的成本上涨，昂贵的医疗保险迫使企业降低薪资涨幅。【原文：Businesses faced some of the sharpest cost increases in recent years in 2025, as more expensive health insurance prompted firms to hand out smaller pay increases, according to a recent survey by the Federal Reserve Bank of New York.】

item.title: 马斯克将就推特投资者指控其欺诈案出庭作证
item.description: 埃隆·马斯克将于周三在陪审团审判中出庭作证，该案指控其在2022年试图回购社交网络公司推特时，通过公开推文操纵该公司股价以谋取更有利的收购条件。【原文：Elon Musk is slated to testify Wednesday in a jury trial over allegations that he manipulated Twitter Inc.’s stock price with his public tweets to try to get himself a better deal to buy the social network back in 2022.】

item.title: 美国服务业活动因需求增长创2022年以来最大增幅
item.description: 美国服务业经济在2月份以2022年中期以来最快的速度扩张，强劲的订单增长和商业活动推动了这一增长。【原文：The US service economy expanded in February at the fastest pace since mid-2022, powered by robust orders growth and business activity.】

item.title: 东南水务因持续停水面临新罚款
item.description: 东南水务有限公司因被调查认定自2020年以来多次引发或加剧供水中断，导致近30万用户受影响，即将面临财务处罚。【原文：South East Water Ltd. is set to face financial penalties after an investigation found it caused or worsened multiple outages that affected nearly 300,000 customers since 2020.】

item.title: 苹果推出599美元MacBook Neo，威胁Windows个人电脑市场
item.description: 苹果公司推出售价599美元的MacBook Neo，这是该公司迄今为止在低端笔记本电脑领域最大规模的扩张举措，旨在争夺注重预算的消费者市场，与Windows个人电脑和Chromebook展开竞争。【原文：Apple Inc. rolled out the $599 MacBook Neo in its biggest push yet into low-end laptops, aiming to challenge Windows PCs and Chromebooks for budget-minded shoppers.】

item.title: 伊朗战争扰乱中东出口，喷气燃料价格飙升
item.description: 随着石油交易商应对伊朗战争的余波，全球航空燃油价格正持续飙升，加剧了人们对全球通胀的广泛担忧。【原文：Jet fuel prices are soaring around the world as oil traders grapple with the fallout of the Iran war, adding to wider concerns about global inflation.】

item.title: 谷歌Gemini被指教唆佛罗里达男子自杀
item.description: 谷歌正面临佛罗里达州一名36岁男子家属的诉讼。该男子据称在该公司Gemini聊天机器人的影响下，曾考虑实施"大规模伤亡袭击"，最终自杀身亡。【原文：Google is facing a lawsuit from the family of a 36-year-old Florida man who allegedly considered carrying out a “mass casualty attack” and ultimately killed himself under the influence of the company’s Gemini chatbot.】

item.title: 美联储官员米兰称尽管伊朗战争爆发仍希望降息
item.description: 美联储理事斯蒂芬·米兰表示，鉴于目前判断中东战争对美国经济的影响为时尚早，他认为继续降息仍是恰当之举。【原文：Federal Reserve Governor Stephen Miran said he thinks it’s still appropriate to continue cutting interest rates given that it’s too early to take a stance on the impact of war in the Middle East on the US economy.】

item.title: 赫格塞特吹嘘对伊朗军方发动"历史性"行动
item.description: 国防部长皮特·赫格塞斯表示，随着伊朗防空系统被摧毁，美以两国即将全面掌控伊朗领空，他同时阐述了加深对伊朗境内目标打击的计划。【原文：Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said the US and Israel are on the cusp of taking complete control of Iran’s airspace as he laid out plans to step up attacks deeper in the country as its defenses are destroyed.】

item.title: 海湾地区航空公司正经历"最糟糕的局面"
item.description: 每家航空公司都有应急预案来应对极端天气或机械故障造成的航班中断，但中东地区的航空公司总会在清单上额外添加一项：战争。【原文：Every airline has contingency plans to manage disruptions from extreme weather or mechanical failures, but those based in the Middle East always have another item on their list: war.】

item.title: 索尼退出PC平台的PlayStation游戏业务
item.description:
这家电子游戏发行商不再计划推出《羊蹄山幽灵》等PlayStation游戏的PC移植版。

【原文：

The video-game publisher no longer plans to put out PC ports of PlayStation games such as ‘Ghost of Yotei’

】

item.title: 欧盟领导人将呼吁采取措施降低电价
item.description: 欧盟领导人将在本月晚些时候的峰会上指示该集团的执行机构提出降低电价的措施，以帮助当地产业。【原文：European Union leaders will direct the bloc’s executive arm to propose measures to lower power prices to aid local industries when they meet at a summit later this month.】

item.title: 俄油价仍低廉 战火威胁全球供应之际
item.description: 尽管中东局势持续升级推动全球油价上涨，俄罗斯旗舰原油混合品种仍以大幅折扣价交易。【原文：Russia’s flagship crude blend is still trading at deep discounts despite a global oil rally driven by the growing escalation in the Middle East.】
